_README: Complete workflow configuration for HappyRobot's Inbound Carrier Sales use case. This maps to the visual workflow editor at platform.happyrobot.ai. Each entry under 'nodes' corresponds to a node you add in the editor.

workflow.name: Inbound Carrier Sales
workflow.description: Automate inbound carrier calls: verify MC authority, match loads, negotiate pricing, transfer to sales rep, then classify and extract call data.

config._how: Go to Assets > Telephony and assign a phone number to this workflow. When a carrier calls this number, the trigger fires and creates a run.

config.prompt: See agent-prompt.md - paste contents into the Prompt field
config.initial_message: Thanks for calling Happy RollBot Freight, this is Sarah. Are you looking to book a load today?
config.tools: See tools-config.json - add 4 tools: verify_carrier, search_loads, evaluate_offer, transfer_call

config.prompt: Classify this inbound carrier sales call based on its outcome. The call is between our AI sales agent Sarah (at Happy RollBot Freight) and a carrier who called in looking to book a load.

tags.name: booked
tags.description: A price was agreed upon and the call was transferred to a sales rep for booking confirmation.

tags.name: carrier_declined
tags.description: The carrier was verified but declined the available loads or walked away from the offered pricing.

tags.name: no_match
tags.description: No loads were found matching the carrier's requested lane, equipment type, or availability dates.

tags.name: verification_failed
tags.description: The carrier's MC number could not be verified - authority revoked, insurance lapsed, or not found in FMCSA.

tags.name: negotiation_failed
tags.description: Negotiation went through all 3 rounds without reaching agreement on price - the carrier's offers were all below our floor.

config.prompt: Classify the carrier's overall sentiment during this inbound sales call. Consider their cooperation level, tone, patience, and satisfaction throughout the conversation.

tags.name: positive
tags.description: Carrier was friendly, cooperative, and seemed satisfied with the interaction even if no deal was made.

tags.name: neutral
tags.description: Carrier was business-like and matter-of-fact. Professional but not particularly warm or cold.

tags.name: negative
tags.description: Carrier showed frustration, impatience, or dissatisfaction with the process, pricing, or load availability.

tags.name: aggressive
tags.description: Carrier was hostile, rude, or confrontational during the call.

config.prompt: Extract key data points from this inbound carrier sales call at Happy RollBot Freight. The call is between our AI agent Sarah and a carrier looking to book a load.

parameters.name: carrier_mc
parameters.description: The carrier's MC number mentioned during the call

parameters.name: carrier_name
parameters.description: The name of the carrier company or the individual calling

parameters.name: load_id
parameters.description: The load ID that was discussed during the call, if any

parameters.name: agreed_rate
parameters.description: The final agreed-upon rate in USD if a deal was reached. Null if no deal.

parameters.name: initial_offer
parameters.description: The carrier's first price offer in USD

parameters.name: final_offer
parameters.description: The carrier's last/final price offer in USD

parameters.name: negotiation_rounds
parameters.description: How many rounds of price negotiation occurred (0-3)

parameters.name: equipment_type
parameters.description: The type of equipment the carrier mentioned having

parameters.name: origin_requested
parameters.description: Where the carrier said they wanted to pick up

parameters.name: destination_requested
parameters.description: Where the carrier said they wanted to deliver

config._how: Add a Webhook POST node. This sends all extracted + classified data to our backend API for the metrics dashboard.

authentication._note: In production, use API Key or Bearer Token auth stored in environment variables

_deployment_notes.1_setup: Create workflow at platform.happyrobot.ai, name it 'Inbound Carrier Sales'
_deployment_notes.2_trigger: Add trigger: select 'Inbound Call', assign a phone number from Assets > Telephony
_deployment_notes.3_agent: Add Inbound Voice Agent node, paste prompt from agent-prompt.md
_deployment_notes.4_tools: Add 4 tools under the agent node per tools-config.json, each with a child Webhook node pointing to your API
_deployment_notes.5_classify: Add two AI Classify nodes for outcome and sentiment
_deployment_notes.6_extract: Add AI Extract node for structured data extraction
_deployment_notes.7_webhook: Add Webhook POST node to log everything to your backend
_deployment_notes.8_test: Test with the web call feature, review transcript in Runs tab
_deployment_notes.10_api_host: Replace YOUR_API_HOST with your deployed backend URL (e.g., your Docker host IP or domain)
